front: Author: Amina disobeys her husband, leaves the house, is hit by a car, and fractures her collarbone
Author: trilogy named Palace Walk, Palace of Desire, and Sugar Street about the al-Jawad family in Cairo
Author: characters representing Moses, Jesus, Muhammad, and modern science live in an alley near their father's mansion
Author: al-Sayyid Ahmad is a philandering patriarch who cheats on his conservative wife Amina
Author: stabbed in the neck at age 82 in 1994 for writing a controversial allegorical novel and supporting Salman Rushdie
Author: son Fahmy is a law student and activist shot dead by British soldiers at a protest
Author: title character seeks revenge after prison, mistakenly kills a doorman and lodger, then is killed by police in a cemetery
back: Naguib Mahfouz (The Thief and the Dogs)

front: Author: Hamida takes the nickname Titi to please English punters and refuses to marry the barber Abbas
Author: Kamal is a possibly autobiographical character who pines after Aida, studies philosophy, and becomes a teacher
Author: Adham is exiled for trying to see his father Gebelawi's forbidden book; Idris tempts him
Author: Arafa the magician represents modern science and causes the death of the title patriarch
Author: set at the title boarding house in Alexandria; multiple narrators entranced with the servant girl Zohra
Author: Abbas throws a beer glass at Hamida's face after discovering her with British soldiers; he is beaten to death
Author: drug addict Anis Zani and friends live on a houseboat on the title river
Author: draws on Sadat's assassination; Randa's engagement to Elwan; 'abyss of the infitah'
back: Naguib Mahfouz (The Day the Leader Was Killed)
work: The Day the Leader Was Killed

front: Author: Jackie Kennedy Onassis oversaw the English translation of his trilogy at Doubleday
Author: spice seller's conjoined twin sons named 'My Fate' and 'My Destiny'
Author: 1979 sequel to 1001 Nights in which Scheherazade distrusts King Shahryar after being spared
Author: only Egyptian and only Arabic-language Nobel Prize in Literature winner
